series.title: [PATCH] hpet: fix bounds check for s->timer[]

review.summary: The patch correctly fixes the off-by-one bounds check in both HPET MMIO timer access paths and also moves the `s->timer[timer_id]` dereference until after validation, eliminating the out-of-range array access. I found no functional regressions or missing companion changes in the modified code.

stages.A: Problem statement matches the diff: `timer_id == s->num_timers` was incorrectly accepted in both read and write handlers.
stages.B: The fix is mechanically correct: changing `>` to `>=` enforces the valid index range `[0, s->num_timers - 1]`.
stages.C: The pointer dereference was moved after the bounds check in both handlers, which is necessary to fully remove the OOB access.
stages.D: The change is minimal and localized; it does not alter valid timer register behavior or tracing for in-range accesses.
stages.E: No additional issues identified from the patch context. With checkpatch clean and configure succeeding, this is ready to merge.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
